title: rhamnus alpina subsp. glaucophylla
intro: Rhamnus alpina subsp. glaucophylla, also known as alpine buckthorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, characterized by its glaucous leaves and small, purple berries. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rhamnus alpina subsp. glaucophylla include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and berries in water or ethanol, and are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
